name: binary_search
theorem:
theorem binary_search_correct (l : List Nat) (x : Nat) :
  Sorted l → (binary_search l x = some i ↔ l[i]! = x) := by sorry
docstring: implement binary search on a sorted array that returns the index of the target element or -1 if not found, prove it always returns the correct index when the element exists

name: insertion_sort
theorem:
theorem insertion_sort_correct (l : List Nat) :
  Sorted (insertion_sort l) ∧ Permutation (insertion_sort l) l := by sorry
docstring: implement insertion sort that sorts a list of natural numbers, prove the output is sorted and is a permutation of the input

name: selection_sort
theorem:
theorem selection_sort_correct (l : List Nat) :
  Sorted (insertion_sort l) ∧ Permutation (insertion_sort l) l := by sorry
docstring: implement selection sort that repeatedly finds the minimum element and places it at the beginning, prove the result is sorted and preserves all elements

name: bubble_sort
theorem:
theorem bubble_sort_correct (l : List Nat) :
  Sorted (insertion_sort l) ∧ Permutation (insertion_sort l) l := by sorry
docstring: implement bubble sort that repeatedly swaps adjacent elements if they are in wrong order, prove it terminates and produces a sorted list

name: merge_sort
theorem:
theorem merge_sort_correct (l : List Nat) :
  Sorted (insertion_sort l) ∧ Permutation (insertion_sort l) l := by sorry
docstring: implement merge sort using divide and conquer, prove it produces a sorted list that is a permutation of the input, prove O(n log n) time complexity bound

theorem:

docstring: implement quicksort with a pivot selection strategy, prove the output is sorted and contains exactly the same elements as the input

docstring: implement radix sort using counting sort as a subroutine for each digit, prove it correctly sorts non-negative integers

theorem:
theorem shell_sort_correct (l : List Nat) :
  Sorted (insertion_sort l) ∧ Permutation (insertion_sort l) l := by sorry
docstring: implement shell sort with diminishing gap sequence, prove the final array is sorted

theorem:
theorem tim_sort_correct (l : List Nat) :
  Sorted (insertion_sort l) ∧ Permutation (insertion_sort l) l := by sorry
docstring: implement a simplified timsort that merges sorted runs, prove the output is sorted and stable

theorem:

docstring: implement introsort that switches from quicksort to heapsort when recursion depth exceeds a limit, prove it always terminates with a sorted array

theorem:
